place: Head Of The Harbor, New York

facts.text: Head of the Harbor became an incorporated village in 1928, marking its formal establishment on Long Island's North Shore.
The village borders Stony Brook Harbor to the north, offering waterfront views that define its coastal character.
Avalon Nature Preserve, a 216‑acre privately owned memorial park with trails, sits on the border with Stony Brook Village.
The village is celebrated for its scenic vistas, winding country roads, waterfront on pristine Stony Brook Harbor, and a preserved sense of history.
A historic village sign warns drivers that roads narrow when entering Head of the Harbor, a cue for careful navigation.
Tree‑lined streets wind through the historic village, giving it a classic New England feel that echoes its early settlement.
